mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 2.5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days whenever this threshold is active.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year period, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates coordinated demand and supply expansion that amplifies earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint effect is stronger than either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive joint growth periods fail to produce higher mean 60-day returns than periods with single-series growth (difference of means t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal accelerating labor market recovery and rising consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This effect dissipates once UNRATE falls below 5%.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return during these declines is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 900, positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in industrial production (INDPRO) confirms rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter increases in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is held constant, negative real interest rates stimulate short-term risk appetite and corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move in this configuration.
failure_condition: Periods of positive CPIAUCSL growth combined with flat or negative FEDFUNDS growth fail to produce positive mean 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) validates aggregate demand strength and reduces recession probability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
